roadmap.notes: Lands the §18 durable-async Workflow primitive (GH #39, J-PLAT-005) over the locked B' direction: distinct Workflow trait + WorkflowCtx (core, no tokio, async_trait), redb-CBOR per-instance journal on the shared ADR-0035 substrate (ADR-0063), async WorkflowEngine driven off the action-shim with reconciler staying pure-sync (ADR-0064), and replay-equivalence as a named DST invariant graduating the ReplayEquivalentEmptyWorkflow placeholder. Three phases = the three carpaccio slices (01 walking skeleton — the heavy slice; 02 ctx.sleep; 03 signals + emit). Slice 01 is decomposed into 6 ordered RED->GREEN->COMMIT steps so each compiles and the acceptance tests it activates can reach GREEN within scope. Every step's test_file + scenario_name is copied VERBATIM from distill/red-classification.md; all 20 scenarios (S-WP-01-01..S-WP-03-05) are covered. HONESTY (enforced, do not violate): journal is redb+CBOR not libSQL (K5); NO `overdrive workflow` CLI verb is invented (#206); NO cross-node resume claimed (#205, single-node only D3); ctx.emit_action goes through the Action channel -> Raft with no IntentStore bypass (slice-03 AC2). Deferrals cite real issues only: cross-node resume #205, CLI verb #206, signals-under-partition #207, journal retention/compaction #208, WASM SDK + version-skew #209.

reviews.scope: DELIVER roadmap coverage critique against distill/test-scenarios.md + red-classification.md + feature-delta.md + slices.
reviews.notes: All 20 scenarios mapped verbatim (test_file + scenario_name exact); zero orphans; S-WP-01-06 walking skeleton in phase 01; dependency DAG compiles in order; honesty constraints (redb+CBOR not libSQL, no CLI verb #206, single-node #205, emit via Action channel->Raft no IntentStore bypass) all verified. Advisory: step 01-06 groups 5 skeleton-closing scaffolds that share one RED->GREEN dependency front — assessed sound, not a sizing violation.

phases.name: Walking skeleton — durable Workflow engine, redb journal, single-node crash-resume
phases.description: Slice 01: the hard core every later slice builds on. Workflow trait + WorkflowCtx (one durable ctx.call) in overdrive-core; JournalStore port + RedbJournalStore + SimJournalStore on the shared redb substrate (ADR-0063); async WorkflowEngine with the journal-cursor replay shape off the action-shim (ADR-0064); pure-sync workflow-lifecycle reconciler bring-up via Action::StartWorkflow; the three DST invariants (replay_equivalence_provision_record graduating the placeholder, WorkflowJournalWriteOrdering, WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume). First consumer ProvisionRecord (real non-idempotent-to-repeat ctx.call effect). Ships end-to-end with the observable ObservationStore terminal row — NOT infrastructure-only.

steps.name: Workflow trait, WorkflowCtx, WorkflowResult, concrete WorkflowSpec in core
steps.description: Create overdrive-core::workflow: Workflow trait (async_trait, async fn run(&self, ctx: &WorkflowCtx) -> WorkflowResult), WorkflowCtx type (injected Clock/Transport/Entropy ports + ctx.call), WorkflowResult enum, concrete WorkflowSpec replacing the placeholder at reconcilers/mod.rs:562. ProvisionRecord impl. No tokio in core; non-determinism flows through ctx only.
steps.criteria: Workflow trait + WorkflowCtx + WorkflowResult + concrete WorkflowSpec exist in core; ProvisionRecord drives run to a terminal WorkflowResult; no tokio in core.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow trait in overdrive-core::workflow uses async_trait: async fn run(&self, ctx: &WorkflowCtx) -> WorkflowResult; the trait declaration pulls no tokio into core (dst-lint passes)
WorkflowResult is a #[non_exhaustive] core enum (Success, Failed{reason}, Cancelled) per ADR-0064 §2, distinct from TerminalCondition; ProvisionRecord drives run to Success in a #[test]
concrete WorkflowSpec replaces the unit placeholder at reconcilers/mod.rs:562; Action::StartWorkflow { spec, correlation } still compiles with the new shape
WorkflowCtx carries injected Arc<dyn Clock/Transport/Entropy> + ctx.call(req) -> Result<Resp> through Transport; the ctx type definition contains no Instant::now/rand/tokio::time (dst-lint clean)
S-WP-01-01 scaffold workflow_trait_drives_to_terminal.rs goes GREEN (drop #[should_panic], add real assertions)
steps.implementation_notes: WorkflowSpec move: the placeholder struct WorkflowSpec; lives in reconcilers/mod.rs:560-562 today because Action carries it; relocate/define the concrete shape in workflow/mod.rs and re-export so Action::StartWorkflow keeps compiling. async_trait is already a core dep (Driver/Transport/Llm use it) — confirm before adding. WorkflowResult inherits the ADR-0037 §5 #[non_exhaustive] + K8s-Condition SemVer convention, NOT the TerminalCondition type. ProvisionRecord is a minimal 2-step (ctx.call -> terminal) sequence.

steps.name: Workflow body purity: no step-machine, all nondeterminism through ctx
steps.description: Land the K6 structural property (AST/grep over the ProvisionRecord workflow body: zero step-enum decls, zero state-transition match arms) and the D-INH-4 nondeterminism check (no Instant::now/reqwest/tokio::time::sleep/rand in the body — only ctx.*). Two scenarios, both author-surface properties over the core trait shape from 01-01.
steps.criteria: K6 AST/grep check confirms zero step-machine boilerplate in a workflow body; a dst-lint-style check confirms nondeterminism routes only through ctx.
acceptance_criteria: K6 metric: an AST/grep check over the ProvisionRecord Workflow impl body asserts zero step-enum declarations and zero state-transition match arms (mechanically verifiable, not free-hand review) per US-WP-1 AC1 / Eclipse H1+L1
S-WP-01-02 scaffold workflow_body_has_no_step_machine.rs goes GREEN

S-WP-01-03: a clean workflow body routes ALL nondeterminism through ctx (positive fn) AND a body smuggling non-ctx nondeterminism is rejected (negative fn) — the dst-lint-style check flags Instant::now/reqwest/tokio::time::sleep/rand outside ctx (D-INH-4 / US-WP-1 AC2)

S-WP-01-03 scaffold workflow_body_routes_nondeterminism_through_ctx.rs goes GREEN for both the positive (clean_workflow_body_routes_all_nondeterminism_through_ctx) and negative (workflow_body_smuggling_non_ctx_nondeterminism_is_rejected) functions
steps.implementation_notes: This step covers two scaffolds (S-WP-01-02, S-WP-01-03); S-WP-01-03 carries a positive + a negative test function (the 29-vs-20 note in red-classification). Both are author-surface property assertions over the trait/ctx shape from 01-01 — no new production component, hence test-only files_to_modify. If the K6 AST check needs a tiny helper it lives in the test module, not production src/.

steps.name: JournalStore port + SimJournalStore (in-memory, injectable fsync-failure)
steps.description: Define the JournalStore port in overdrive-control-plane::journal (append, load_journal, probe) per ADR-0063. JournalEntry CBOR enum (ciborium): Started/CallResult/Terminal for slice 01, additive #[serde(default)]. SimJournalStore in overdrive-sim with an inject_fsync_failure handle. Records inputs/digests, never derived caches.
steps.criteria: JournalStore trait + JournalEntry CBOR enum defined; SimJournalStore round-trips losslessly and records inputs not derived state; inject_fsync_failure handle exists.
acceptance_criteria: JournalStore async trait (Send+Sync) in overdrive-control-plane::journal: append (one fsync'd write, ADR-0063 §4), load_journal (range scan (id,0)..=(id,u32::MAX) -> ordered Vec<JournalEntry>), probe (Earned-Trust sentinel write->fsync->readback->delete); typed thiserror JournalStoreError + ProbeError

JournalEntry is a CBOR (ciborium) #[serde(...)] enum with slice-01 variants Started{spec_digest,input_digest}, CallResult{step,correlation,response_digest}, Terminal{result}; additive evolution via #[serde(default)] (ADR-0063 §2) — no rkyv envelope, no libSQL

SimJournalStore in overdrive-sim::adapters::journal stores CBOR bytes in a BTreeMap<(WorkflowId,u32),Vec<u8>> (NOT HashMap); proptest round-trip: append then load_journal returns byte-equal ordered entries
SimJournalStore::inject_fsync_failure() makes the next append return JournalStoreError without persisting (used by the ordering invariant in 01-06); clear_fsync_failure() resets

journal entries record INPUTS/digests (spec_digest, response_digest), never a derived deadline/remaining cache (development.md Persist inputs, not derived state) — asserted; S-WP-01-05 scaffold journal_records_inputs_not_derived.rs goes GREEN
steps.implementation_notes: Mirrors ViewStore/SimViewStore almost line-for-line (ADR-0063 Negative consequence: shapes copy-adaptable). JournalEntry lives co-located with JournalStore. ciborium is already in the graph from ADR-0035 — no new dep. Both port + sim adapter behind #[allow(dead_code)] until the engine (01-05) wires them. SimJournalStore in src/adapters/mod.rs + lib.rs re-exports. UPSTREAM FIX (DELIVER 01-03, orchestrator-authorized): step 01-01 placed ProvisionRecord inline in the workflow_trait_drives_to_terminal.rs test scaffold, which is not reachable from overdrive-sim. Promote ProvisionRecord into overdrive-core::testing (gated #[cfg(any(test, feature="test-utils"))], the existing pattern — overdrive-sim already dev-deps overdrive-core with features=["test-utils"]) so the sim journal test can construct it; update the 01-01 test to import the promoted fixture instead of its inline copy (keep 01-01 GREEN).

steps.name: RedbJournalStore on the shared redb substrate (real-IO, integration-tests)
steps.description: RedbJournalStore sharing the RedbViewStore Arc<Database> and redb file per ADR-0063 §1: one __wf_journal__ table, key (WorkflowId,u32), value CBOR JournalEntry. append = one Durability::Immediate write; load_journal = range scan; probe writes a sentinel. Real-redb integration test asserts the CallResult is present and no libSQL table exists (K5/O6).
steps.criteria: RedbJournalStore shares the redb file/handle, appends durably, range-scans on load, probes per Earned-Trust; the call result is read back from real redb and no libSQL journal exists.
acceptance_criteria: RedbJournalStore::new takes the shared Arc<Database> (same file as RedbViewStore — ADR-0063 §1 one-file-two-layouts); the __wf_journal__ table name uses the reserved prefix outside the ReconcilerName grammar so it cannot collide with a reconciler table

append executes ONE redb write with Durability::Immediate (fsync before return, ADR-0063 §4); load_journal(id) range-scans (id,0)..=(id,u32::MAX) returning entries in step order; probe writes the (__probe_wf__,0) sentinel, fsyncs, reads back byte-equal, deletes, leaving no state on success

integration test (under tests/integration/workflow_journal/, integration-tests feature, real fsync via Lima): write a ProvisionRecord CallResult, read it back from the real redb journal byte-equal; grep/dep-graph confirms NO libSQL journal table (K5/O6, US-WP-2 AC1)
S-WP-01-04 scaffold journal_writes_to_redb.rs goes GREEN; runs via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests
dst-lint passes (adapter-host crate, not core); cargo xtask mutants on src/journal/redb.rs achieves kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: Precedent: tests/integration/redb_view_store.rs (sibling real-redb ViewStore test). Wire the mod integration { mod workflow_journal { mod journal_writes_to_redb; } } subtree in tests/integration.rs. ADR-0063 §1: redb begin_read/begin_write both take &self so the shared Arc<Database> is safe. The probe is part of the boot composition-root 'probe view-store AND journal-store, then use' invariant (wired in 01-05).

steps.name: WorkflowEngine with journal-cursor replay; action-shim dispatches off the shim
steps.description: WorkflowEngine in overdrive-control-plane::workflow_runtime (async, tokio task set): on start, load_journal, build WorkflowCtx with the replay buffer + cursor, drive run(&ctx).await; each ctx.* is check-then-record (ADR-0064 §3). Replace the no-op Action::StartWorkflow arm at action_shim/mod.rs:446 with WorkflowEngine::start, off the shim. On terminal, write the ObservationStore terminal-result row keyed by CorrelationKey.
steps.criteria: The engine drives run with the cursor replay shape; the action-shim hands StartWorkflow to WorkflowEngine::start off the shim (not run as a reconciler); terminal row written via the sanctioned path.
acceptance_criteria: WorkflowEngine::start(spec, correlation) takes Arc<dyn JournalStore> + injected ports mandatorily (no builder default); load_journal then spawn run(&ctx) as a tracked tokio task; the cursor replay shape (ADR-0064 §3): replay (cursor<len) returns recorded entry without re-firing the effect, live (cursor==len) performs the effect + appends with fsync BEFORE suspend + advances cursor

action_shim/mod.rs:446 no-op arm Action::StartWorkflow { .. } => Ok(()) is REPLACED with a dispatch to WorkflowEngine::start, off the shim exactly as Action::StartAllocation -> Driver::start (ADR-0064 §5, DDD-5 — the RATIFY-flagged engine<->reconciler boundary); the workflow body is NOT run as a per-tick reconcile loop

on run returning a WorkflowResult, the engine writes a terminal-result ObservationStore row keyed by the instance CorrelationKey via the shim's ObservationStore write path (NOT a direct engine write bypassing the channels) — slice-01 AC5
S-WP-01-11 scaffold action_shim_dispatches_start_workflow_to_engine.rs goes GREEN (the start action is dispatched to the engine off the shim, not run as a reconciler)
cargo xtask mutants on src/workflow_runtime + the action_shim StartWorkflow arm achieves kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: ADR-0064 §5 is the subtlest boundary (DDD-5, RATIFY-flagged): the engine is to workflows what Driver is to allocations — driven off the action-shim (ADR-0023's sanctioned async boundary), NOT a reconciler. The action_shim:446 arm currently groups Noop | StartWorkflow | HttpCall as no-dispatch; split StartWorkflow out into its own arm calling WorkflowEngine::start. Composition root (overdrive-cli) must probe both view-store AND journal-store at boot before the first workflow starts (ADR-0063 §4); refusing startup on probe failure with health.startup.refused is part of the engine wiring. CORE EXTENSION (orchestrator-authorized, per ADR-0064 §1/§3): the 01-01 WorkflowCtx fires ctx.call directly with no replay/record — §3 check-then-record needs WorkflowCtx to carry a CORE-LEVEL journal-cursor-handle trait (an async_trait the ENGINE implements in control-plane over Arc<dyn JournalStore> + cursor state; core stays tokio-free, JournalEntry stays in control-plane). Extend overdrive-core/src/workflow/mod.rs with that handle trait + a WorkflowCtx field + ctx.call rewritten to: replay (handle yields recorded CallResponse → return without firing) vs live (fire Transport → handle records+fsync+advances → return). Update the 01-01 acceptance test for the new ctx constructor and keep it GREEN. The journal CallResult variant (journal/mod.rs, from 01-03) likely must carry the replay-able CallResponse value (slice-01 bytes_sent), not only a response_digest, so replay returns the recorded response byte-equal — additive #[serde(default)] field, keeps 01-03/01-04 GREEN. If any breakage falls outside these listed files, STOP and report.

steps.name: Engine terminal row + lifecycle reconciler re-hydrate (pipeline live)
steps.description: Make the end-to-end pipeline observable: add ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal (keyed by CorrelationKey) and have the engine write it via the sanctioned shim path on run terminal (slice-01 AC5); wire the real WorkflowEngine into dispatch (replacing the None left by 01-05). Add the pure-sync workflow-lifecycle reconciler (ADR-0035 shape) that re-emits Action::StartWorkflow for a running instance with no live engine task on restart (ADR-0064 §5).
steps.criteria: Engine writes a terminal-result ObservationStore row via the shim path; the pure-sync lifecycle reconciler re-hydrates a running instance on restart and ReconcilerIsPure still holds.
acceptance_criteria: ObservationRow gains a WorkflowTerminal variant (keyed by CorrelationKey, carrying the WorkflowResult terminal) per ADR-0064 §2; the additive variant's exhaustive-match arms are filled across all in-crate consumers (mechanical ripple, authorized)

on run returning a WorkflowResult the engine writes the terminal-result ObservationStore row keyed by the instance CorrelationKey via the shim's ObservationStore write path — NOT a direct engine write bypassing the channels (slice-01 AC5)

the real WorkflowEngine is wired into action-shim dispatch (replacing the Option None passed by reconciler_runtime in 01-05) so a committed StartWorkflow actually drives run off the shim at boot composition

the workflow-lifecycle reconciler (pure-sync, ADR-0035 shape, in overdrive-core::reconcilers + control-plane registration) re-emits Action::StartWorkflow for an instance running-in-intent with no live engine task on restart; the engine's load_journal then resumes rather than cold-starts (US-WP-3 AC4)
ReconcilerIsPure still holds with the workflow-lifecycle reconciler registered; S-WP-01-08 scaffold lifecycle_reconciler_rehydrates_on_restart.rs goes GREEN
steps.implementation_notes: Split out of the original 01-06 (orchestrator, after 01-05 surfaced the ObservationRow exhaustive-enum ripple + engine-terminal-write + dispatch-wiring scope). The lifecycle reconciler state lands in overdrive-core::reconcilers (an AnyState variant) + control-plane registration per ADR-0064 §1 — it does NOT know cold-start vs crash-resume; the engine's load_journal decides (keeps reconcile clean). The ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal additive variant forces exhaustive-match arms across existing consumers (overdrive-core/control-plane/sim) — those mechanical ripple edits are AUTHORIZED even though the touched files are not all enumerable up-front; if a non-mechanical change is needed outside the listed files, STOP and report. Engine terminal write reuses the shim's ObservationStore write path (same path the StartAllocation arm uses), NOT a bypassing direct write. This step makes the pipeline live + observable; 01-07 then asserts on it via the DST invariants. SINGLE-NODE only (D3/#205).

steps.name: Three workflow DST invariants + walking-skeleton crash-resume
steps.description: Graduate the ReplayEquivalentEmptyWorkflow placeholder into ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord (real journal replay against engine + SimJournalStore: uninterrupted vs crash-resumed byte-equal trajectory + assert_eventually!(is_terminal) bounded progress, K4); add WorkflowJournalWriteOrdering and WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume. Lands the @walking_skeleton crash-resume S-WP-01-06 over the live pipeline from 01-06.
steps.criteria: The three workflow DST invariants are named (no inline literal), green on the cargo dst critical path, seed-reproducible; the walking-skeleton single-node crash-resume is demonstrated exactly-once.
acceptance_criteria: S-WP-01-06 (@walking_skeleton): under DST, kill the instance AFTER ctx.call records but BEFORE terminal, restart -> the ctx.call effect executes EXACTLY ONCE (SimTransport call count == 1) and the resumed WorkflowResult is byte-identical to the uninterrupted run (K1/K3, O1/O4); scaffold workflow_crash_resume_exactly_once.rs GREEN
S-WP-01-07: a committed step is read back from the journal and the run resumes from the first unrecorded await (K2/O2, single-node); scaffold workflow_committed_step_survives_crash.rs GREEN

S-WP-01-09 (K4 load-bearing): ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord is a NAMED overdrive-sim Invariant variant (no inline literal) graduating ReplayEquivalentEmptyWorkflow; real journal replay (uninterrupted vs crash-resumed byte-equal) + assert_eventually!(is_terminal); prints a seed, reproduces bit-for-bit; on the cargo dst critical path; scaffold replay_equivalence_provision_record_invariant.rs GREEN

S-WP-01-10: WorkflowJournalWriteOrdering invariant — SimJournalStore::inject_fsync_failure on the next append -> the engine does NOT advance the cursor or suspend with an unrecorded step (mirrors ADR-0035 WriteThroughOrdering); WorkflowExactlyOnceEffectOnResume also added; scaffold workflow_journal_write_ordering.rs GREEN
the observable terminal asserted by the WS scenario reads the ObservationRow::WorkflowTerminal row from 01-06 (correlation_key linkage); SINGLE-NODE only — no AC claims cross-node resume (D3/#205)
steps.implementation_notes: Graduates the placeholder: mod.rs:136 ReplayEquivalentEmptyWorkflow variant -> ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord; evaluators.rs:584 evaluate_replay_equivalent_empty_workflow (two-SimEntropy-transcript stub) -> real journal replay driving the WorkflowEngine (from 01-05/06) + SimJournalStore; harness.rs:404 wiring. The DST invariant drives the engine to-terminal on the sim executor (await the run future under turmoil) — exactly-once via the §3 replay cursor (no transport re-fire on resume). All four sim scaffolds reach GREEN together over the live pipeline from 01-06. cargo mutants on the evaluators is DEFERRED to Phase 5 (do not run per-step). SINGLE-NODE only (D3/#205) — no AC claims cross-node resume.

phases.name: Durable ctx.sleep across a crash
phases.description: Slice 02: add ctx.sleep(Duration) as a durable await-point routed through the injected Clock (SimClock parks on a deadline; harness advances logical time). Additive SleepArmed{step,deadline_unix} journal entry (records the DEADLINE input, not 'remaining' — development.md Persist inputs, not derived state). Extend the consumer to ctx.call -> ctx.sleep -> ctx.call. Extend the replay invariant to hold across the sleep. Smaller than slice 01 (one additive ctx method + one additive journal variant + invariant extension).

steps.name: ctx.sleep through Clock; SleepArmed journal variant records deadline
steps.description: Add ctx.sleep(Duration) to WorkflowCtx routed through the injected Clock; live writes the SleepArmed{step,deadline_unix} additive CBOR journal entry (the deadline, an input) then parks on the SimClock deadline. Resume recomputes remaining wait from recorded_deadline - clock.now(). Covers the inputs-not-derived journal scenario.
steps.criteria: ctx.sleep routes through Clock and journals the deadline (input), not a remaining-duration cache; the SleepArmed variant is additive CBOR.
acceptance_criteria: ctx.sleep(Duration) added to WorkflowCtx routed through the injected Arc<dyn Clock> (SimClock parks on a deadline, harness advances logical time); no tokio::time::sleep in core (dst-lint clean)
SleepArmed{step,deadline_unix} added to the JournalEntry enum additively via #[serde(default)] (ADR-0063 §2) — no version bump, no golden fixture; CBOR not rkyv

the sleep-armed entry records the DEADLINE (an input); resume recomputes remaining wait as recorded_deadline - clock.now() — no persisted 'remaining ms' field (development.md Persist inputs, not derived state; slice-02 AC4)
S-WP-02-03 scaffold workflow_sleep_records_deadline_not_remaining.rs goes GREEN
ProvisionRecord (or a slice-02 consumer) extended to a ctx.call -> ctx.sleep -> ctx.call 3-await shape
steps.implementation_notes: The ctx.sleep live path: write SleepArmed (fsync) then suspend parking on the Clock deadline (ADR-0064 §3 live branch for sleep). Replay path: ctx.sleep returns immediately if the recorded deadline has passed. The journal-cursor machinery from slice 01 is reused unchanged — this is purely an additive ctx method + entry variant (ADR-0064 §4).

steps.name: Sleep crash-resume invariants + replay-equivalence across the sleep
steps.description: Extend the DST surface for the sleep: crash during the sleep window does not repeat the pre-sleep step (SimTransport count); post-sleep step fires only at/after the ORIGINAL deadline regardless of crash timing (SimClock); and replay-equivalence holds across the sleep, seeded and reproducible. Extends the named replay invariant rather than adding a new family.
steps.criteria: Crash spanning the sleep window does not repeat the pre-sleep step; the post-sleep step honours the original deadline; replay-equivalence holds across the sleep.
acceptance_criteria: S-WP-02-01: under DST, crash during the sleep window -> on resume the pre-sleep ctx.call executes exactly once (SimTransport call count == 1) (K1/O1); scaffold workflow_sleep_crash_pre_sleep_step_not_repeated.rs GREEN

S-WP-02-02: the post-sleep ctx.call fires only at/after the ORIGINAL recorded deadline, regardless of when the crash occurred (asserted via SimClock advancing logical time) (K3/O4); scaffold workflow_sleep_resumes_to_original_deadline.rs GREEN
S-WP-02-04: the replay-equivalence invariant holds across a durable sleep, seeded and reproducible bit-for-bit (K4/O5); scaffold replay_equivalence_holds_across_sleep.rs GREEN
the ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord invariant (or its slice-02-extended evaluator) exercises the ctx.call -> ctx.sleep -> ctx.call shape on the cargo dst critical path
cargo xtask mutants on the extended sleep evaluators achieves kill rate >= 80%
steps.implementation_notes: Extends the existing ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord evaluator (from 01-06) to drive the 3-await sleep shape, rather than minting a new invariant family — slice briefs say 'extend an existing named DST invariant'. SINGLE-NODE only (D3/#205). Production wall-clock fidelity is OUT (slice-02 brief): DST drives logical time; production uses SystemClock, same Clock trait, no extra surface.

phases.name: Typed signals + workflow-to-cluster Action emission
phases.description: Slice 03: ctx.wait_for_signal(SignalKey) on a typed ObservationStore signal (journaled await-point; resume re-checks whether the signal already arrived) and ctx.emit_action(Action) routing a typed cluster mutation through the SAME Action channel the reconciler runtime consumes -> Raft, never a direct IntentStore write (development.md Workflow contract rule 6). Additive SignalAwaited/SignalSeen/ActionEmitted CBOR variants. Extend the DST invariant across a signal wait + emit. In-process single-node signal delivery only (#207).

steps.name: ctx.wait_for_signal + ctx.emit_action; additive journal variants
steps.description: Add ctx.wait_for_signal(SignalKey) (ObservationStore signal rows; SignalAwaited/SignalSeen additive CBOR variants) and ctx.emit_action(Action) (routes through the Action channel -> Raft; ActionEmitted additive variant) to WorkflowCtx per ADR-0064 §4. emit_action lands the typed Action in the Raft channel with NO direct IntentStore write. Covers the emit-lands-in-channel scenario.
steps.criteria: ctx.wait_for_signal and ctx.emit_action exist; emit_action lands in the Action channel -> Raft with no IntentStore bypass; signal/emit journal variants are additive CBOR.
acceptance_criteria: ctx.wait_for_signal(SignalKey) -> SignalValue added to WorkflowCtx reading typed signals from the ObservationStore; SignalAwaited{step,signal_key} + SignalSeen{step,signal_key,value_digest} added to JournalEntry additively via #[serde(default)] (ADR-0063 §2)

ctx.emit_action(Action) added routing through the SAME Action channel the reconciler runtime consumes -> Raft (whitepaper §18 Primitive Composition; development.md Workflow contract rule 6 — no Raft bypass); ActionEmitted{step,action_digest} added additively

S-WP-03-03: ctx.emit_action lands the typed Action in the Action/Raft channel AND the workflow makes NO direct IntentStore write — asserted (US-WP-5 AC2, slice-03 AC2); scaffold workflow_emit_action_lands_in_raft_channel.rs GREEN
ctx does NOT expose a .put() on IntentStore (development.md Workflow contract rule 6); the emit goes only through ctx.emit_action
no tokio/Instant/rand smuggled into core; CBOR additive variants only (no rkyv, no libSQL)
steps.implementation_notes: Two additive ctx methods + three additive journal variants on the slice-01 machinery (ADR-0064 §4). Signal delivery is in-process single-node via the ObservationStore (#207 cross-node-under-partition is OUT). The emit reuses the existing Action channel -> Raft commit path the reconciler runtime already uses; ActionEmitted makes the emit idempotent on resume (covered by 03-02).

steps.name: Signal + emit crash-safety invariants + replay-equivalence
steps.description: Extend the DST surface for signals/emit: crash while blocked on a signal re-blocks on the SAME signal on resume; a signal seen before the crash is not re-waited; an Action emitted before the crash is not re-emitted on resume (ActionEmitted journal entry makes emit idempotent); and replay-equivalence holds across a signal wait + an emit, seeded and reproducible.
steps.criteria: Blocked-on-signal re-blocks on the same signal after crash; a seen signal is not re-waited; an emitted Action is not re-emitted; replay-equivalence holds across signal+emit.
acceptance_criteria: S-WP-03-01: crash while blocked on ctx.wait_for_signal -> on resume the workflow blocks on the SAME signal, no duplicate downstream effect (K1/O1, US-WP-5 AC1); scaffold workflow_signal_wait_reblocks_after_crash.rs GREEN
S-WP-03-02: a signal seen before the crash (SignalSeen recorded) is NOT re-waited on resume — replay returns the recorded value (O1); scaffold workflow_signal_already_seen_not_rewaited.rs GREEN
S-WP-03-04: an Action emitted before the crash (ActionEmitted recorded) is NOT re-emitted on resume (K1/O1, US-WP-5 AC3); scaffold workflow_emit_action_not_re_emitted_after_crash.rs GREEN

S-WP-03-05: the replay-equivalence invariant holds across a signal wait + an emit, seeded and reproducible bit-for-bit (K4/O5, US-WP-5 AC4); scaffold replay_equivalence_holds_across_signal_and_emit.rs GREEN
cargo xtask mutants on the extended signal/emit evaluators achieves kill rate >= 80%; single-node only (#207)
steps.implementation_notes: Extends the ReplayEquivalenceProvisionRecord evaluator to drive the signal-wait + emit shape (slice brief: 'extend the DST invariant'). The check-then-record replay shape (ADR-0064 §3) makes both the signal-wait and the emit idempotent on resume: replay reads SignalSeen / skips a recorded ActionEmitted rather than re-firing. In-process single-node signal delivery only (#207).
